title: Disegno Test: Who Are You in the Age of AI?
description: Operator, Curator, or Designer? Seven questions – no right or wrong, but a mirror of your stance toward design.

questions.text: You're starting a new design project. Your first move is:

options.text: Open Figma, Photoshop, or another tool – I experiment directly on screen.
ChatGPT or Midjourney – I have AI generate variations first.
Pen and paper – I sketch the core idea before opening any tool.
I research the cultural context and formulate criteria first.

questions.text: AI generates an impressive image from your prompt. You think:

options.text: Perfect, I can use this directly.
Good starting point – I'll refine the prompt for better results.
Technically good, but what was my original intention?
The result lacks a point of view. Beautiful, but arbitrary.

questions.text: What makes a good designer in your eyes?

options.text: Someone who masters the tools perfectly and delivers fast.
Someone who recognizes and selects the best option from many.
Someone who sees what others don't – and sets a direction.
Someone who creates meaning, not just surfaces.
A nice hobby, but not necessary for professional design.
A quick way to communicate ideas.
A way of thinking – I understand things by drawing them.
Something AI will soon do better than me.

questions.text: The biggest change AI brings to design:

options.text: Finally less boring production work.
More variations to choose from.
The question of WHAT to create becomes more important than HOW.
Now everyone can be a designer – pure democratization.

questions.text: An empty sheet of paper is for you:

options.text: Intimidating – I need a template or reference.
A starting point – I gather input and inspiration first.
A thinking space where relations become visible and assertions take shape.
Unnecessary – I work directly on screen.

questions.text: When machines can execute everything, the most valuable skill becomes:

options.text: Speed and efficiency.
Good taste and the ability to select the best.
The quality of the first setting – judgment, vision, cultural depth.
Prompt engineering – whoever writes the best instructions wins.

entwerfend.title: Designer – Designator
entwerfend.description: You think before you execute. You set direction before opening tools. AI becomes your workshop – you provide the vision, the machine unfolds it. You stand in the tradition of Disegno: design as cognition, not production.
entwerfend.motto: First the stroke, then the answer.

kurator.title: Curator
kurator.description: You select, arrange, and refine. You recognize quality and make good decisions among variants. AI amplifies your ability to curate – but the Atelier invites you to also set your own direction. From selecting to designing is just one step.
kurator.motto: Don't just choose the best – set something new.

operator.title: Operator
operator.description: You define yourself through tool mastery and technical perfection. That was the standard for decades – but AI is now taking over exactly this work. This is not an attack on you. It's an invitation to discover what design means beyond software.
operator.motto: The machine takes over the machine. What remains is you.
